    <h1 {...{
      "id": "apis-usage"
    }}>{`APIs Usage`}</h1>
    <p>{`This document provides information on how to use the Messaging APIs to receive and display an Inbox UI in your application.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "importing-messaging"
    }}>{`Importing Messaging`}</h2>
    <p>{`To use the Messaging APIs, you need to import the Messaging module in your Swift file.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`import AEPMessaging
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "apis"
    }}>{`APIs`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "getinboxui"
    }}>{`getInboxUI`}</h3>
    <p>{`Use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`getInboxUI`}</inlineCode>{` method to obtain an `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "./public-classes/inboxui.md"
      }}>{`InboxUI`}</a>{` instance for a given surface and display a SwiftUI Inbox view with the qualified content cards.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "parameters"
    }}>{`Parameters`}</h4>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><em parentName="li">{`surface`}</em>{` - The `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`Surface`}</inlineCode>{` for which to retrieve the inbox.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><em parentName="li">{`customizer`}</em>{` - An optional `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../content-card-ui/iOS/public-classes/contentcardcustomizing.md"
        }}>{`ContentCardCustomizing`}</a>{` object to customize the appearance of the content cards within the inbox. If you do not need to customize the appearance of the content cards, this parameter can be omitted.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><em parentName="li">{`listener`}</em>{` - An optional `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "./public-classes/inboxeventlistening.md"
        }}>{`InboxEventListening`}</a>{` object to listen to state and interaction events from the inbox. If you do not need to listen to events, this parameter can be omitted.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <blockquote>
      <p parentName="blockquote"><strong parentName="p">{`Note`}</strong>{` - This API returns an `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxUI`}</inlineCode>{` immediately. The inbox will not have content until `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`updatePropositionsForSurfaces`}</inlineCode>{` has been called with the same surface. Call `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`updatePropositionsForSurfaces`}</inlineCode>{` to download content from Adobe Journey Optimizer prior to or after calling this API.`}</p>
    </blockquote>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "syntax"
    }}>{`Syntax`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`@available(iOS 15.0, *)
public static func getInboxUI(for surface: Surface,
                              customizer: ContentCardCustomizing? = nil,
                              listener: InboxEventListening? = nil) -> InboxUI
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "example"
    }}>{`Example`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`// Create a surface matching your Adobe Journey Optimizer campaign configuration
let inboxSurface = Surface(path: "inbox")

// Get the InboxUI instance
let inboxUI = Messaging.getInboxUI(for: inboxSurface)

// Display the inbox view in SwiftUI
struct InboxPage: View {
    var body: some View {
        inboxUI.view
            .onAppear {
                Messaging.updatePropositionsForSurfaces([inboxSurface])
            }
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "example-with-listener-and-customizer"
    }}>{`Example with Listener and Customizer`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`let inboxSurface = Surface(path: "inbox")

let inboxUI = Messaging.getInboxUI(
    for: inboxSurface,
    customizer: MyCardCustomizer(),
    listener: self
)
`}</code></pre>
    <hr></hr>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "updatepropositionsforsurfaces"
    }}>{`updatePropositionsForSurfaces`}</h3>
    <p>{`Dispatches a network request to fetch propositions for the provided surfaces from Adobe Journey Optimizer. Call this API to download the latest content cards and inbox settings before displaying the inbox.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "syntax-1"
    }}>{`Syntax`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`public static func updatePropositionsForSurfaces(_ surfaces: [Surface])
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "example-1"
    }}>{`Example`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`let inboxSurface = Surface(path: "inbox")
Messaging.updatePropositionsForSurfaces([inboxSurface])
`}</code></pre>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "typical-usage-flow"
    }}>{`Typical Usage Flow`}</h2>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Register and configure the AEPMessaging extension at app launch.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Call `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`Messaging.updatePropositionsForSurfaces`}</inlineCode>{` to download inbox content from the server.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Call `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`Messaging.getInboxUI(for:)`}</inlineCode>{` to obtain an `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`InboxUI`}</inlineCode>{` instance.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Display the inbox using the `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`InboxUI.view`}</inlineCode>{` property in your SwiftUI or UIKit view.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Optionally assign a listener and customizer to respond to events and style the inbox.`}</li>
    </ol>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`import SwiftUI
import AEPMessaging

struct InboxPage: View {
    let inboxUI: InboxUI

    init() {
        let surface = Surface(path: "inbox")
        inboxUI = Messaging.getInboxUI(for: surface)
        inboxUI.isPullToRefreshEnabled = true
    }

    var body: some View {
        NavigationView {
            inboxUI.view
                .navigationTitle("Inbox")
        }
        .onAppear {
            Messaging.updatePropositionsForSurfaces([Surface(path: "inbox")])
        }
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
